etymology.description: semantic 行(行) (xíng, "to go; walk") + phonetic 圭(圭) (guī, "jade tablet") -> street
etymology.interpretation: The character 街(街) (jiē, "street") combines the semantic element 行(行) (xíng, "to go; walk"), which suggests movement or travel, with the phonetic element 圭(圭) (guī, "jade tablet"), which gives the sound. The notion of a 'street' emerges from the idea of a path or way where people would walk or travel.

examples.chinese: 她每天早上沿着这条街跑步(她每天早上沿著這條街跑步)。
examples.pinyin: Tā měitiān zǎoshang yánzhe zhè tiáo jiē pǎobù.
examples.english: She jogs along this street every morning.

examples.chinese: 这条街上有许多商店(這條街上有許多商店)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè tiáo jiē shàng yǒu xǔduō shāngdiàn.
examples.english: There are many shops on this street.

examples.chinese: 他们在街对面开了一家新餐馆(他們在街對面開了一家新餐館)。
examples.pinyin: Tāmen zài jiē duìmiàn kāile yī jiā xīn cānguǎn.
examples.english: They opened a new restaurant across the street.
